metrics.primary: CR answer accuracy (deterministic upstream match-mode, judge-free)

coverage.note: all four competencies now measured with the P67-C task-specific answer harness: CR 0.959 (70/73), AR 0.853 (81/95), TTL 0.767 (23/30, up from 0/30), LRU 0.518 (29/56, up from 1/6). Deterministic upstream match-mode scoring, no LLM judge.

claimBoundary.reason: Strongest honest candidate of all benchmarks: deterministic, judge-free (no same-model bias), and the P67-C task-specific harness lifts every competency past target (CR 0.959, AR 0.853, TTL 0.767, LRU 0.518). CR ~0.96 is the cleanest claim and beats the published single-hop ceiling ~0.60. REMAINING BLOCKERS for a public claim: (1) no in-harness baseline reproduced (the ~0.60 ceiling is external, not run here) so metrics.baseline is null; (2) executionFailures 5, not 0 — the failures are transient shared-gateway drops on long sequential runs (15/15 clean in isolation), so a clean run needs resumable retry infra (the BEAM live-slice pattern), not a harness change. REQUIRED DISCLOSURES if ever promoted: MAB measures answer-formatting / current-value resolution strength, NOT retrieval — 66% of correct CR, 68% of correct AR, and 100% of correct TTL answers had no full gold evidence retrieved (mean evidence recall CR 0.57 / AR 0.27 / TTL 0.02); AR is a multiple-choice leak (the gold next-event is a prompt candidate) so it must never be headlined as retrieval; TTL was a fixable answer-format artifact (0 -> 0.767 from the classification harness alone); LRU (0.518, evidence recall 0.68) is the most retrieval-coupled but remains hard.
